data.title: New Zealand's Gentrack climbs on new business for g2 platform
data.description:
** Shares of Gentrack Group up 5.2% at NZ$7.89, set for their strongest session since August 14, if current trends hold

** The utilities provider saysits g2 platform selected to enhance operations and customer experience at Pennon Water Services, one of the UK's leading business water and wastewater retailers

** Gentrack's g2 platform is a solution designed to modernize operations, reduce cost-to-serve, and enable rapid innovation for energy and water companies

** About 525,441 shares change hands, 4.6x the 30-day average

** YTD, stock down 37.4%, including the day's moves

data.title: Indonesia to establish legal framework for establishing trusts
data.description:
Indonesia's Ministry of Finance is seeking to establish a legal framework for special purpose vehicles covering asset securitisation and managers of trust funds, it said late on Thursday.

Indonesia has a civil law system and did not have rules for trusts until a new law was introduced in 2023.

The finance ministry's new regulation will lay out detailed rules based on the 2023 law, which was intended to deepen Indonesia's financial markets and attract investors to the country, it said in a statement.

Rules on trustees will adopt characteristics commonly used in countries with common law systems, including the separation of legal ownership and beneficial ownership, as well as the principle of bankruptcy remoteness, which ensures that managed assets remain separate from the insolvency risks of the party depositing the assets, the statement read.

"This regulation aims to provide stronger legal protection and enhance confidence in asset management in Indonesia," the ministry said.

After the regulation comes into effect, the trustee model can be used by existing entities, including sovereign wealth funds and the private sector, it said.

The ministry said a consultation process and technical discussions were underway, without providing a time frame for when the new rules would be applied.

data.title: Hindalco Industries shares in focus after reports of another fire at Novelis' Oswego plant
data.description:
Shares of Aditya Birla Group's Hindalco Industries Ltd. will be in focus on Friday, November 21, after reports of another fire having taken place at its unit Novelis' Oswego plant last month.

There was a major fire that took place in September, while a smaller fire was reported last month as well.

Novelis and its results were an overhang on Hindalco and its financial performance recently. Novelis in its earnings statement projected a cash flow hit between $550 million to $650 million and a loss of $100 million to $150 million on the Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) front.

While Hindalco is anticipating a recovery of 70% to 80% of the amount through insurance, Novelis mentioned that there are plans to restart operations at the affected part of the plant by the end of December.

On a standalone basis for the September quarter, Hindalco's revenue increased by 9.5% from last year to ₹24,264 crore.

EBITDA for the quarter grew by 14% from last year, while margins expanded by 50 basis points. EBITDA for both Aluminium and Copper business were ahead of expectations.

Shares of Hindalco ended 1.2% lower on Thursday at ₹800.1. The stock trades near its 52-week high of ₹864.

data.title: SNG: EDGE Group, Leonardo move towards setting up JV
data.description:
Staff Writer

Abu Dhabi-based EDGE Group has announced that it has concluded a preliminary assessment of technology transfer activities with Leonardo, an Italian aerospace and defence company, thus confirming its intention to set up a joint venture in Abu Dhabi.   

Following this latest development, the parties will work to launch the JV in 2026. The latest agreement was signed at Dubai Airshow 2025.

EDGE Group would own 51% of the JV, with Leonardo clinching the remaining 49%. The activities being considered include design, development, testing, industrialisation and production, sale/lease and through-life support and training for the JV’s products in the UAE, IP right licensing, and the creation of a skilled locally based workforce. 

The JV’s products will be marketed in the UAE, and through the UAE to selected international export markets. The portfolio under consideration will be based on a range of Leonardo’s solutions covering various business areas spanning sensors, system integration, and platforms, it stated.

Hamad Al Marar. Managing Director and CEO of EDGE Group, said: "The latest milestone on the path towards the full establishment of a landmark joint venture between EDGE and Leonardo underscores not only the speed with which we are moving, but highlights what can be achieved when two industry players collaborate on the development of a smart strategy to deliver technological excellence for global security."

"By collaborating across several critical domains including air, land, sea, and electro-optics, we can tailor solutions born from solid experience, expertise, and innovation, through the UAE to existing and new untapped markets," he added.

Roberto Cingolani, CEO and General Manager of Leonardo, said: "This latest milestone, which follows months of intense work between the partners, testifies our mutual understanding of the added value we can create paving the path for an even stronger collaboration."

"The combination of our capabilities in the UAE, in some premium technology fields we’ve identified for an in-depth evaluation, can deliver highly competitive solutions to the reference market. We look forward to continuing to work towards the next stage of cooperation," he added.-TradeArabia News Service

Copyright 2025 Al Hilal Publishing and Marketing Group Provided by SyndiGate Media Inc. (Syndigate.info).

data.title: Japan's cabinet approves lavish $135 stimulus as markets fret over fiscal policy
data.description:
Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's cabinet approved a 21.3 trillion yen ($135.40 billion) economic stimulus package on Friday, marking the first major policy initiative under the new leader, who has pledged to pursue expansionary fiscal measures.

The package includes general account outlays of 17.7 trillion yen, far exceeding the previous year's 13.9 trillion yen and representing the largest stimulus since the COVID pandemic. It will also include 2.7 trillion yen in tax cuts.

Growing concerns about the nation's worsening fiscal position brought about by Takaichi's lavish stimulus package have sent the Japanese currency to 10-month lows and super-long government bond yields to record highs.

The package will be funded by an expected rise in overall tax revenue reflecting inflation, as well as an additional issuance of government bonds.

The size of additional government bond issuance has still to be finalised, but is expected to be larger than the 6.69 trillion yen issued for last year's stimulus, sources familiar with the matter said.

The cabinet plans to approve a supplementary budget to fund the package as early as November 28, aiming to secure parliamentary approval by the end of the year.

($1 = 157.3100 yen)

data.title: Plantro offers $272 million to take Dye & Durham private, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Plantro, controlled by former Dye & Durham CEO Matt Proud, has proposed to buy the legal software company for about C$384 million ($272.59 million), Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar.

The report added that the offer was sent to Dye's special committee this week to pay about C$5.72 apiece in cash and notes.

The offer includes C$3.50 in cash at closing and C$2.22 in senior unsecured notes yielding 15%, with partial interest payable in cash, the report said.

Dye's second-largest shareholder, Plantro, told the board it is ready to sign a binding agreement immediately with no further due diligence and offered a go-shop period for competing bids, the report added.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. Plantro did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment, while Dye & Durham declined to comment.

Last month, Plantro dropped its previous bid for Dye & Durham and said it might cut its 11% stake in the legal software maker, citing weak results and a lack of board engagement.

The Toronto-listed company at the time rebuffed Plantro's statements, saying they were inaccurate and added that the company is committed to a strategic review.

($1 = 1.4087 Canadian dollars)

data.title: Indonesia Stocks Edge Lower But Still Set for Weekly Gain
data.description:
Indonesia’s stocks slipped 15 points, or 0.2%, to 8,410 in Friday morning trade, reversing gains from the past two sessions amid weakness in healthcare, logistics, and transport.

Sentiment softened after a sharp selloff on Wall Street, as U.S. tech losses and fading hopes for a December Fed rate cut weighed on risk appetite.

Traders were cautious about adding new positions, partly due to concerns over financial strain in several sectors domestically, especially coal, where net profits have plunged about 67% from their 2022 peak, according to a think tank.

Still, the IDX is on track for a weekly gain of around 0.5% after earlier weakness, lifted by Bank Indonesia’s decision to keep rates at 4.75% for a second meeting in November.

A Q3 2025 current account surplus, the first since Q1 2023, also bolstered external stability and helped reinforce confidence in the rupiah.

Transcoal Pacific plunged 4.6%, along with Cisarua Mountain Dairy (-4.4%), Medco Energi (-2.3%), and Indosat (-3.3%).

data.title: Singapore Manufacturing Sector's Growth Set to Moderate Next Year — Market Talk
data.description: The performance of Singapore's export-oriented manufacturing sector seems set to moderate next year despite robust performance over 1Q-3Q, says DBS senior economist Chua Han Teng in a report. The electronics segment has been resilient, supported by U.S. tariff exemptions on electronics imports, as well as solid AI-related demand. However, the current electronics upcycle, which has seen a period of strong growth, could stabilize eventually. This would occur if the AI boom somewhat wanes and threatened U.S. semiconductor tariffs come into effect. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Fitch Affirms Quzhou Qutong Transportation Investment Development at 'BBB-'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Hong Kong-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Quzhou Qutong Transportation Investment Development Co., Ltd.'s (QQTI) Long-Term Foreign- and Local-Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) at 'BBB-'. The Outlook is Stable.

We regard QQTI as a strategically important government-related entity (GRE) of Quzhou municipality in China's Zhejiang province. The rating affirmation and Stable Outlook reflect our view that a shareholding change in mid-2025 will unlikely dilute the municipal government's ultimate ownership or effective control of the company nor weaken QQTI's policy role in Quzhou's urban and transport development and the government's financial support for its mission.

The ratings are derived from our assessment of the Quzhou government's responsibility and incentive to provide support to QQTI under our Government-Related Entities Rating Criteria. We look through QQTI's immediate shareholder to the ultimate government sponsor following the shareholding change, as the company has a policy role and is closely controlled by the government sponsor. The immediate shareholder is a holding company, and QQTI's management team also serves as the holding company's management.

The ratings also consider the company's 'b' Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) that is assessed under our Public Policy Revenue-Supported Entities Rating Criteria.

KEY RATING DRIVERS Support Score Assessment:'Extremely likely'

We believe extraordinary support from the Quzhou municipal government to QQTI would be extremely likely if needed, reflecting a support score of 35 (out of a maximum 60) under Fitch's GRE criteria. This reflects a combination of responsibility and incentive to support factor assessments.

Responsibility to Support Decision Making and Oversight:'Very Strong'

The assessment reflects QQTI's ultimate state ownership and the Quzhou government's control and oversight of the company's key personnel appointments, operational activities and major corporate events. The board of directors is the highest decision-making body, with members directly appointed by the municipal government. The government is also responsible for supervising the implementation of the company's significant investment projects, such as the Intelligence Industrial Park, which is designated as the city's education industry cluster.

In mid-2025, Quzhou State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission (SASAC) approved the injection of equity in QQTI held by Quzhou State-owned Capital Operation Co., Ltd. and Quzhou Holdings Group Co., Ltd. into QQTI's subsidiary Quzhou Xin'an Holdings Co., Ltd. The latter company was subsequently renamed Quzhou Qutong Holdings Group Co., Ltd. (QQHC) and became QQTI's immediate sole shareholder. Despite the change in shareholding, Quzhou SASAC remains effectively in control of QQTI, while QQTI's management also serves as QQHC's management.

Precedents of Support:'Strong'

The Quzhou government has historically provided strong support to QQTI through cash equity injections, operating subsidies, special-purpose bond (SPB) proceeds, ultra-long treasury bond proceeds and debt swaps. Government cash payments totalled about CNY850 million in 2024 (2023: CNY273 million), of which about CNY70 million were subsidies that were equal to 11% of QQTI's EBITDA. The company also received government-mandated share transfers of CNY23 million in 2024. We expect the support to continue, enabling QQTI to fulfil its public policy mandate.

Incentives to Support Preservation of Government Policy Role:'Strong'

QQTI remains Quzhou's key investment, construction and maintenance platform for transportation, which is strategically important to the region's economic development and social stability. We believe a default by the company would interrupt financing to projects under construction and preservation of public services such as road maintenance. While substitutes may prevent a halt of key services, it will be difficult to replace QQTI in the long term. This is because those substitutes operate with different remits and lack the intensive expertise that QQTI has developed over the past two decades.

Contagion Risk:'Strong'

QQTI is a high-profile GRE for the Quzhou government, given its policy role and leading position in the city's transportation development. The new holding company is unlikely to displace QQTI's role or its financing function in executing its mandated mission in the coming years. Key public projects continue to be implemented through QQTI, with assets and debt largely tied to these mandates. QQTI reported total debt of CNY18.9 billion at end-2024, most of which was raised to fund transport-related projects. We will reassess this key rating factor if the immediate shareholder materially expands its involvement in the financing of policy projects.

Standalone Credit Profile

The Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) of 'b' is based on the combination of a 'b' financial profile, 'Midrange' risk profile and peer comparison. The notch-specific SCP is at 'b' because QQTI's forecast net leverage ratio of 32.9x by 2029 is comparable to the average of peers with 'b' SCPs. QQTI's high leverage profile is counterbalanced by the company's ample liquidity and strong access to funding, as well as expected government support.

Risk Profile::'Midrange'

Fitch assesses QQTI's risk profile as 'Midrange', reflecting 'Midrange' revenue, expenditure, and liabilities and liquidity risks.

Revenue Risk::'Midrange'

QQTI's revenue prospects are tied to Quzhou's steady growth and supported by diversification into sectors such as hydropower, industrial park leasing, construction, property development and commodities, which should reduce concentration risk. Its pricing profile is aided by the education industrial park's favorable leasing margins, with a shift towards self-operating, self-sustaining projects helping to offset low-flexibility, policy-driven segments like railways.

Expenditure Risk::'Midrange'

QQTI has well-identified cost drivers with moderate volatility. The company has been engaged in transport and urban development for over two decades and has sufficient expertise, management experience and local resources. QQTI's major investment and funding plans have to follow municipal government guidelines and require oversight and approval by the government.

Liabilities and Liquidity Risk::'Midrange'

QQTI's adjusted debt rose to CNY20.4 billion by end-2024 but growth has slowed to low double digits amid restrictions on bond issuance for many policy-driven GREs in China, with increasing support from fiscal allocations, SPB and ultralong treasury bonds. The company's debt carries a medium-term profile (5.4-year weighted-average debt maturity). The share of short-term debt rose to 35% of total debt in 2024, from 31% in 2023, but this is mitigated by QQTI's strong refinancing ability.

Contingent liabilities remain manageable despite modest increases. Liquidity is ample, with near-term maturities of CNY6.6 billion covered by unrestricted cash of CNY2.4 billion and uncommitted undrawn bank facilities of CNY8.8 billion at end-2024. While non-standard financing rose to CNY2.1 billion to offset constrained bond issuance, we expect this exposure to be contained under municipal government oversight.

Financial Profile:'b'

QQTI's adjusted net debt/EBITDA rose slightly to 29.3x in 2024 driven by debt-funded investments. We estimate its leverage will rise to 32.9x by end-2029, in view of its investment mandates to support Quzhou's transport and urban construction and its business development needs that will partly rely on additional debt.

Peer Analysis

QQTI's closest peer is Quzhou Huicheng Industrial Development Group Co., Ltd. (Huicheng, BBB-/Stable). QQTI and Huicheng have distinct mandates assigned by the Quzhou government. QQTI focuses on transport projects and developing industrial parks for the education sector, while Huicheng is responsible for general industrial park development such as the Smart New City District. Both are equally important to the government sponsor, leading to comparable levels of oversight and support. Each has sound funding access underpinned by strong bank relationships, which could amplify contagion risk in the event of a default.

Issuer Profile

QQTI is 98.2% ultimately owned by the Quzhou SASAC via Quzhou Qutong Holdings Group Co., Ltd. The company's key policy mandates include transportation project construction and investment, road maintenance and industrial park construction and operation. It also has commercial activities, including commodity sales, engineering construction and property development.

Key Assumptions

Fitch's rating case is a through-the-cycle scenario, which incorporates a combination of revenue, cost and financial risk stresses. It is based on 2020-2024 historical figures and 2025-2029 scenario assumptions:

- average operating revenue growth of 7.9% a year, driven by Quzhou's demand growth prospects;

- average operating expenditure growth of 8% a year, reflecting a stable cost structure;

- net capex (capex minus capital revenue) to average about CNY2.2 billion a year to support the city's development needs;

- net adjusted debt to reach about CNY29.1 billion based on estimated funding needs, counterbalanced by earning ability and fiscal support.

Rating Sensitivities Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

-- A lowering of Fitch's credit view of Quzhou municipality's ability to provide support or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations;

-- A deterioration in Fitch's perception of the Quzhou municipality's responsibility or incentive to provide support that lowers the support score assessment to below 35 points.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

-- An improvement of Fitch's credit view of the Quzhou municipality's ability to provide support or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations;

-- An improvement in Fitch's perception of the Quzhou municipality's responsibility or incentive to provide support that raises the support score assessment to 45 points or above.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

References for Substantially Material Source Cited as Key Driver Rating The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria.

Quzhou Qutong Transportation Investment Development Co., Ltd.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; BBB-; Rating Outlook Stable

----; Local Currency Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; BBB-; Rating Outlook Stable

data.title: Japan's biggest nuclear power awaits key decision from regional governor
data.description:
By Katya Golubkova

A Japanese regional governor is expected to say on Friday whether he will allow a partial restart of the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa nuclear power plant, the world's biggest, as Japan tries to revive its nuclear sector and reduce fossil fuel imports.

Approval by Niigata Prefecture Governor Hideyo Hanazumi would remove the last major hurdle for plant operator Tokyo Electric Power Co (TEPCO) to go ahead with plans to restart one or two of Kashiwazaki-Kariwa's biggest reactors.

A restart would be the first for TEPCO since the March 2011 tsunami destroyed its Fukushima Daiichi power plant. It would also be a breakthrough for Japan, which after the disaster shut all 54 nuclear reactors in operation at the time, leaving it heavily reliant on fossil fuel imports vulnerable to production shocks and supply disruptions.

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, who took office last month, has said she supports more nuclear relaunches to strengthen energy security and to address the cost of imported energy, which accounts for 60% to 70% of Japan's electricity generation.

"The restart ... is extremely important from the perspective of reducing power supply and electricity prices, and securing decarbonized power sources," Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said on Friday.

Of the 54 reactors in operation pre-Fukushima, Japan has restarted 14 of the 33 that remain operable.

Governor Hanazumi is expected to announce his decision at 4 p.m. (0700 GMT) on Friday. It was not immediately clear whether his decision would be affected by a report on Thursday by Japan's nuclear regulator about inadequate management of confidential security documents at the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant.

TEPCO, if approval is granted for both, plans to restart units No. 6 and No. 7, which together can produce 2,710 megawatts of electricity, or about a third of Kashiwazaki-Kariwa's total capacity of 8,212 MW. TEPCO has said it plans to decommission some of the other five units at the facility.

In July, Kansai Electric Power , Japan's top nuclear power operator, said it would begin conducting surveys to investigate building a reactor in western Japan that would be the first new unit since the Fukushima disaster.

TEPCO shares were down 1.3% on Friday, paring losses after the Nuclear Regulation Authority's report on Thursday and performing better than a 2.3% drop in the Nikkei index .

data.title: Finance minister says Thai economy is stable, sees Q4 growth picking up
data.description:
The Thai economy is very stable and government stimulus measures will give growth a boost in the final quarter of 2025, the finance minister said on Friday.

Inflation and unemployment were low, Ekniti Nitithanprapas told a business forum, adding the government will keep the public debt ratio below 70% of gross domestic product.

The government is also planning a package of measures to support smaller businesses, including soft loans, he added.

Southeast Asia's second-largest economy, which has lagged peers since the pandemic, has faced multiple headwinds this year, including U.S. tariffs, high household debt, and a strong baht .

Economic growth was just 1.2% on an annual basis in the third quarter, the weakest pace in four years.

data.title: Brent Hits 4-week Low
data.description:
Brent decreased to 62.55 USD/Bbl, the lowest since October 2025.

Over the past 4 weeks, Brent Crude Oil gained 0.02%, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 16.72%.

data.title: Foreign investment in Indian stocks set to rebound, says asset manager Amundi
data.description:
Key points:
  • Foreign investors sold $16.4 billion in shares so far in 2025
  • Strong domestic demand offers hedge against global volatility
  • Nifty 50NSEIup 10.8% this year, lags broader Asian peers

By Vivek Kumar M

Amundi , Europe's largest asset manager, expects foreign investors to return to Indian equities as heavy selling in 2025, which caused the world's fastest-growing major economy to underperform other emerging markets, begins to ease.

Foreign investors have sold $16.4 billion of Indian shares so far this year, marking their second-largest outflow on record, even as domestic institutional buyers stepped in with $77 billion of purchases.

The outflows, driven by soft nominal growth, moderate earnings, steep U.S. tariffs, and high valuations left Indian equities trailing broader emerging-market benchmarks for the first time in five years.

India's benchmark index Nifty 50 has risen 10.8% so far in 2025 and is within 0.5% of last year's record high, but it has lagged behind a 24% gain in MSCI's Asia-Pacific ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) and a 16% rise in China's Shanghai Composite .

With valuations nearing neutral zone and domestic demand strong, conditions for a rebound in foreign flows are falling into place, said Alessia Berardi, head of emerging macro strategy at Amundi Investment Institute. The Paris-based firm manages $2.67 trillion in assets.

"Real GDP was strong in April–June, but nominal growth was only in the high single digits, which is weak for India, where it's typically above 10%," Berardi said.

Growth should normalize in the coming quarters as corporate earnings improve, she said.

"India will continue to outperform even in the medium term. We recommend increasing structural allocation to overweight Indian equities," she said, citing India as one of Amundi's top emerging market picks.

Amundi remains "slightly positive" on Indian and broader emerging market equities for the first half of 2026, while it holds a "neutral" stance on China, Japan, and U.S. equities.

INDIA AS A HEDGE AGAINST GLOBAL VOLATILITY

Berardi said Indian equities offer a useful hedge in a volatile global backdrop, supported by the economy's heavy reliance on domestic demand rather than exports.

High U.S. tariffs are not "really a concern", she added.

India's limited exposure to global trade tensions and U.S. tariffs provides a buffer for investors, with consensus expectations still pointing to potential tariff reductions later this year.

Recent policy steps to spur consumption, including expectations around the next scheduled central government pay revision cycle in India, further support the outlook.

Despite a rally in China, India is increasingly viewed as a diversifier and middle-power growth story with stronger medium-term prospects, Berardi said.

($1 = 88.6900 Indian rupees)

data.title: Japan Inflation, Exports Could Firm Rate-Hike Hopes — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Data showing persistent inflation in Japan and exports holding firm in the face of tariffs could tilt the scales toward a rate hike sooner rather than later.

Steady price growth and resilient demand for Japanese goods could be encouraging signs for the Bank of Japan, which has been waiting for the right time to resume its tightening cycle.

Despite persistent inflation, the central bank has maintained its policy interest rate at 0.5% since its last hike in January as it looks for clarity on the full impact of tariffs on Japan's economy.

Another key part of the puzzle is the pace of wage growth, which has struggled to keep up with prices.

Friday's data showed that Japan's consumer inflation picked up slightly in October.

Excluding volatile fresh food prices, inflation increased 3.0% from a year earlier last month, up slightly from September and matching the increase expected in a poll of economists by data provider Quick.

"With inflation excluding fresh food and energy remaining above 3%, it won't be long before the Bank of Japan resumes its tightening cycle," said Marcel Thieliant, head of APAC at Capital Economics.

The October print can be particularly significant as that is when the second half of the fiscal year begins and Japanese companies tend to review prices for products and services.

The BOJ watches pricing behavior closely to gauge willingness to pass on costs to customers as it looks to help create steady consumption and growth.

A recent survey by research firm Teikoku Databank showed that 195 major food-and-beverage makers opted to bump up price tags for over 3,000 items in October. That is up 3.4% from a year earlier, marking the 10th consecutive month of increases.

But some economists say price growth may slow in the coming months as the government rolls out a planned, large-scale economic package that includes inflation-relief measures such as energy subsidies. The government is expected to approve the plan later in the day.

Another issue lies in the fact that inflation remains overwhelmingly supply-driven, said Stefan Angrick at Moody's Analytics.

"With nominal pay gains sputtering, evidence of demand-driven inflation is scarce," said Angrick, head of Japan and frontier markets economics.

While government support for households should help temper price pressures, he expects inflation to ease only gradually, in part due to the yen's weakness. That's a challenge for the BOJ, as raising rates to support the yen risks choking fragile domestic demand, he added.

"We expect the central bank will hike interest rates in January, but a hike could come before year's end."

Economists at ING view the data as backing the case for a December rate hike, but they also flagged the possibility that government pressure for accommodative monetary policy could postpone a move until next year.

Exports growth coming in stronger than expected despite U.S. tariffs supports resuming monetary tightening, wrote ING senior economist Min Joo Kang.

Separate data released Friday confirmed that Japan's overall exports remain resilient, as a weak yen boosted the value of Japanese goods sold overseas, helping offset the impact of U.S. tariffs.

Outbound shipments from Japan increased 3.6% from a year earlier, following September's 4.2% rise. The latest print exceeded the 1.1% consensus rise compiled by data provider LSEG.

The yen averaged 149.51 against the dollar in October, 2.5% weaker than a year earlier.

A weak yen inflates the overseas earnings of Japanese exporters when repatriated. The pair last stood at 157.39.

Shipments to the U.S. dropped 3.1% on the year last month due to weakness in autos, chip-making machines and pharmaceuticals, but while that marked a seventh straight month of contraction, it was much better than September's 13.3% fall.

Still, worries remain about the longer-term impact of U.S. trade policy.

Even though Washington and Tokyo reached a trade agreement earlier this year, Japanese companies still face 15% duties on their exports that threaten to hurt profits. A squeeze to corporate profits could ripple out across the economy, crimping wage growth and draining confidence.

If upcoming data shows more evidence of a tariff-driven hit to Japan's economy, that could complicate policymaking at the Bank of Japan.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: TCS-TPG AI data centre deal draws upbeat calls but JPMorgan cites concerns of muted 5-6 year impact
data.description:
Shares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Ltd. will be in focus on Friday, November 21, after the Tata Group giant signed an agreement with TPG Terabyte Bidco to invest up to ₹18,000 crore in HyperVault.

HyperVault, a wholly owned subsidiary of the company, will build AI-ready data centres with a capacity of over 1 GW.

TPG, one of the world's leading alternative asset managers, will invest up to ₹8,820 crore as part of the deal.

Under the structure, TCS and TPG will invest in a 51:49 ratio, with TPG expected to hold between 27.5% and 49% in HyperVault.

The investment will be infused through CCPS in multiple tranches over the next few years. Both sides will be subject to a three-year lock-in.

TCS will retain board control, while TPG will be allowed up to two board seats.

Brokerage firm JPMorgan has maintained its 'Overweight' rating on TCS with a price target of ₹4,050.

The brokerage said that TPG's commitment of up to $1 billion reduces TCS's equity burden and that the company expects synergies from global partnerships with hyperscalers and AI firms.

JPMorgan, however, remains cautious on the synergy narrative and believes the investment is unlikely to materially alter shareholder returns over the next five to six years.

Morgan Stanley has also kept its 'Overweight' call with a price target of ₹3,430.

The brokerage said the participation of a private equity investor at this scale reinforces the long-term potential of the HyperVault business.

Of the 51 analysts that have coverage on TCS, 36 have a 'Buy' rating on the stock, 10 say 'Hold', while five have a 'Sell' rating.

It sees room for further announcements tied to this vertical, adding that investors may gradually begin valuing this business using a sum-of-the-parts approach.

data.title: Palm dips on weaker rival oils, softer exports; still set for second weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures edged lower on Friday, tracking weaker rival edible oils, crude oil prices and softer November export data.

The benchmark palm oil contract for January delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid 9 ringgit, or 0.22%, to 4,146 ringgit ($1,000.97) a metric ton in early trade.

The contract has so far risen 0.51% this week and remains on track for a second weekly gain.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 1.21%, while its palm oil contract shed 1.42%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were down 0.31%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Oil prices extended their decline for a third straight session as the U.S. pushed for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal that could bring more oil supplies onto the global market, while uncertainty over U.S. interest rate cuts curbed investor risk appetite. O/R

* Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, strengthened 0.26% against the dollar, making the commodity more expensive for buyers holding foreign currencies.

* Cargo surveyors estimated that exports of Malaysian palm oil products for November 1-20 fell between 14.1% and 20.5% from a month earlier.

* India's palm oil imports are expected to rebound by nearly 20% in the new marketing year, driven by competitive pricing that is helping the tropical oil regain market share.

* Thousands of residents in Indonesia's Riau province palm oil belt protested against the takeover of their plantations by the government's forestry task force.

* Palm oil may fall into a range of 4,076 ringgit to 4,102 ringgit per metric ton, driven by a wave (5), Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said. TECH/C

MARKET NEWS

* Asian shares extended a global rout as the much-anticipated U.S. jobs data failed to provide clarity on the near-term path for interest rates, with investors returning to dumping risk assets even after Nvidia's earnings dazzled. MKTS/GLOB

DATA/EVENTS

0700 UK Retail Sales MM, YY Oct

0700 UK Retail Sales Ex-Fuel MM Oct

0815 France HCOB Mfg, Svcs, Comp Flash PMIs Nov

0930 UK Flash Mfg, Svcs, Comp PMIs Nov

1445 US S&P Global Mfg, Svcs, Comp PMIs Flash Nov

1500 US U Mich Sentiment Final Nov

($1 = 4.1420 ringgit)
